channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: No Dem effort to stymie Trump election win certification: Strategists
item.description: A few Democratic lawmakers say they will accept a free and fair Trump election win, but aren't sure he'll be fair or honest.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Venice Beach home buried in sand after Milton
item.description: While most people are dealing with water in their homes, some homeowners are battling an inundation of sand.
item.dc:creator: Austin Kellerman, WGN 9

item.title: 3 Europeans engineered $95M Texas Lotto win with over 25M tickets
item.description: It was successful for one group last year, but Texas Lottery officials have made changes to inhibit anyone else from trying it.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Elevator door was 'broken' during Colorado mine tour accident that killed 1 and trapped 12
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Investigators on Friday were trying to figure out what led to an elevator accident inside a former Colorado gold mine that killed a tour guide, injured four others and left a separate group of 12 people trapped for hours at the bottom of the tourist attraction 1,000 feet (305 meters) b
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN, AMY BETH HANSON, MEAD GRUVER and JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: TikTok was aware of risks kids and teens face on its platform, legal document alleges
item.description: TikTok was aware that its design features are detrimental to its young users and that publicly touted tools aimed at limiting kids’ time on the site were largely ineffective, according to internal documents and communications exposed in lawsuit filed by the state of Kentucky.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trial on hold for New Jersey man charged in knife attack that injured Salman Rushdie
item.description: MAYVILLE, N.Y. (AP) — The attempted murder trial of the man charged with severely injuring author Salman Rushdie in a 2022 knife attack was put on hold Friday while judges consider a request to move it to another county. Jury selection had been scheduled to start on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 7-Eleven to close over 400 'underperforming' locations in North America
item.description: The company cited slow sales, declining traffic, inflationary pressures, and a decrease in cigarette purchases among the reasons why some stores are closing.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: 3 in 5 millennials want full access to abortion: Study
item.description: The largest percentile of individuals who do not want to live in a "fully legal" abortion state is Gen Z at 33%, followed by Gen X and Baby Boomers (30%).
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: If you let your flood insurance lapse and then got hit by Helene, you may be able to renew it
item.description: Residents in states hit by Hurricane Helene who had coverage through the federal flood insurance program but let it lapse before the storm hit may be able to renew it and still be covered from the impact.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Santana, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: SEE IT: Oregon police find bag of narcotics ironically labeled ‘Definitely not a bag full of drugs'
item.description: Police in Portland, Oregon, found a bag full of drugs, that ironically had “Definitely Not a bag full of Drugs" printed on the outside of it.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chicago Marathon to take place on Sunday
item.description: The Chicago Marathon will take place on Sunday, October 13, with thousands of runners and spectators expected to attend, and several downtown streets closures already in place.
item.dc:creator: Angelica Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Florida deputies help pregnant woman in labor during Milton
item.description: Polk County Sheriff Grady Judd said that some of the office's deputies helped transport a pregnant woman who was going into labor to the hospital early Thursday morning, as Hurricane Milton brought widespread power outages, high winds and flooding to Central Florida.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KRON4

item.title: Experts warn 'crazy busy' Atlantic hurricane season is far from over
item.description: Millions of people in the southeastern U.S. still are reeling from the catastrophic damage caused by Hurricanes Helene and Milton, but scientists warn that the Atlantic hurricane season is far from over. “As far as hurricane landfalls in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ISABELLA O'MALLEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: WATCH: Former blue state governor, stepson brutally beaten by band of teens
item.description: Curtis Sliwa posted brutal footage of a band of teens kicking and punching his son, Anthony Sliwa, and his son's stepfather, former New York Gov. David Paterson, in New York City's Upper East Side.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 1 killed, 23 rescued after equipment malfunction at tourist gold mine in Colorado
item.description: One person was killed and 23 were rescued after being trapped for about six hours inside a former Colorado gold mine when an elevator malfunctioned at the tourist site, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, Fox 5

item.title: Hurricane misinformation causing misery for survivors: Abrams
item.description: Radio caller tells Dan Abrams a relative believes that if he accepts hurricane relief from FEMA, the government could take his house.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Internet Archive data breach exposes more than 31 million user accounts: reports
item.description: The Internet Archive, a popular digital library known for its Wayback Machine, was hacked and suffered a breach that reportedly exposed the data of 31 million user accounts.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Condemned inmate Richard Moore wants someone other than South Carolina's governor to decide clemency
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — A South Carolina inmate scheduled to be executed in just over three weeks is asking a federal judge to take away the power of granting clemency from the governor who is a former state attorney general and place it with a parole board.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Prosecutors reviewing 'new evidence' in Marilyn Manson sexual abuse probe
item.description: The musician and artist, whose real name is Brian Warner, has vehemently denied the allegations, though he has settled at least two related lawsuits.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, NewsNation

item.title: Immigrants brought to the US as children ask judges to keep protections against deportation
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Immigrants who built lives in the United States after being brought here illegally as children were among more than 200 demonstrators who gathered Thursday outside a federal courthouse in New Orleans, where three appellate judges heard arguments over the Biden administration's pol
item.dc:creator: KEVIN McGILL and JACK BROOK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hurricane Milton spawned high number of destructive, deadly tornadoes
item.description: FT. PIERCE, Fla. (AP) — Dozens of tornadoes spawned by Hurricane Milton caught many Floridians by surprise as they braced for heavy rain, strong winds and storm surges. Violent twisters were seen crossing highways, ripping off roofs and downing trees and power lines.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANY MATAT, ISABELLA O'MALLEY and SUMAN NAISHADHAM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Chicago man charged with assaulting 2 officers during protests of Netanyahu address to Congress
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An Illinois man was arrested Thursday on charges that he attacked two police officers during protests in Washington, D.C., against Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s address to Congress in July.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: WATCH: Cruise ship battles rough seas in Gulf of Mexico during hurricane
item.description: Gulf of Mexico (Oct. 10, 2024) — The Harmony of the Seas, a Royal Caribbean cruise ship, faced rough seas in the Gulf of Mexico on October 8, 2024, as Hurricane Milton disrupted its scheduled voyage.
item.dc:creator: Brody Wooddell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Rare comet visible in the night sky won't return for 80,000 years
item.description: A rare comet is expected to be visible to the naked eye across the Northern Hemisphere for several weeks in October.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, KRON4

item.title: 14-year-old found floating on fence in Tampa floodwaters after Hurricane Milton
item.description: A 14-year-old had to be rescued after Hurricane Milton's floodwaters trapped him on a floating fence in the middle of Tampa.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, KRON4

item.title: Over 135 rescued from Tampa assisted living facility amid 'unprecedented' flooding from Milton
item.description: “This is extraordinary to see this type of flooding, especially in this type of area,” said Hillsborough County Sheriff Chad Chronister.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Atlantic City mayor and his wife plead not guilty to beating their daughter
item.description: MAYS LANDING, N.J. (AP) — The mayor of Atlantic City and his wife, who is the seaside gambling resort's schools superintendent, pleaded not guilty Thursday to beating and abusing their teenage daughter, with a lawyer saying that “parenting struggles are not criminal events.” Mayor Marty Small Sr.
item.dc:creator: WAYNE PARRY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: CNN's Anderson Cooper hit by flying object during Milton live shot
item.description: CNN’s Anderson Cooper was hit by a flying object on Wednesday during a live shot while reporting from Florida on Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Although Milton has moved on, at least 8 dead and millions in the dark
item.description: Hurricane Milton barreled into the Atlantic Ocean on Thursday after plowing across Florida, where it knocked out power to more than 3 million customers and whipped up a barrage of tornadoes.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Video: Wind tears roof off Tropicana Field, home of the Rays
item.description: Hurricane Milton made landfall in Siesta Key south of St. Petersburg, and while the city avoided some of the worst storm surges that doesn't mean the city avoided major effects from the storm.
item.dc:creator: Garrett Phillips, Fox 5

item.title: Taylor Swift donates $5 million to Hurricane Helene and Milton relief efforts
item.description: “This contribution will help communities rebuild and recover, providing essential food, clean water, and supplies to people affected by these devastating storms," Feeding America CEO Claire Babineaux-Fontenot said.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: NC National Guard helicopter crew grounded after 'rotor wash' incident blows away Helene aid
item.description: Video of the incident appears to show the wind from the helicopter blowing over tents and scattering items in the air.
item.dc:creator: Rodney Overton, WGN 9

item.title: Watch: Massive 28-foot wave captured inside Hurricane Milton
item.description: Drone footage captured a monstrous wave inside Hurricane Milton as the powerful storm passed through the Gulf of Mexico on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, Fox 5

item.title: Tampa's 'Lt. Dan' says he's riding out Hurricane Milton on boat despite mayor's warning
item.description: A Tampa man who goes by the nickname "Lt. Dan" has caused a flurry of online concern for his safety during Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, Fox 5

item.title: Lawyers: Sean 'Diddy' Combs seeks trial next April or May on sex trafficking charges
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Sean ‘Diddy’ Combs wants to go to trial on sex trafficking charges next spring, lawyers for the jailed hip-hop mogul told a judge on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Kremlin confirms Trump sent COVID tests to Putin during pandemic
item.description: The Kremlin confirmed that former President Trump while in office sent Russian President Vladimir Putin COVID-19 testing devices during the height of the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, ABC 4

item.title: Raccoon sparks chaos in stands during Broncos game against Raiders
item.description: Football fans packed Empower Field in Denver on Sunday to watch the Broncos take on the Las Vegas Raiders, but they weren’t the only ones in attendance.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Rhodes, ABC 4
